title: Bird Watching Trail – Bangalore
venue: Organized by CARE Wildlife Wing, Charlie’s Animal Rescue Centre
caption_full:
Bird Watching Trail – Bangalore
Organized by CARE Wildlife Wing, Charlie’s Animal Rescue Centre
Join us for an exciting Bird Watching Trail in Bangalore and experience the fascinating world of birds in their natural habitat. This trail is designed for nature enthusiasts, bird lovers, photographers, students, and anyone interested in exploring urban biodiversity.
Participants will have the opportunity to observe and learn about a variety of bird species, their behavior, feeding habits, ecological roles, and identification techniques. The trail will be guided by experienced birders who will share interesting facts and insights about the birds encountered during the walk.
Whether you are a beginner or an experienced birder, this event offers a wonderful chance to connect with nature, improve your bird identification skills, and understand the importance of conserving bird habitats.
📅 Date: Sunday, 7th June
⏰ Time: 6:30 AM – 10:30 AM
📍 Location: Bangalore (Exact location will be shared upon registration)

title: Bird Walk: Real & Imaginary Birds
caption_full:
Bird Walk: Real & Imaginary Birds 🦅🎨
Join us for a Nature Explorers bird walk at Lalbagh! Discover how birds survive in different habitats, explore their amazing adaptations, and learn to look at birds through the lens of habitat and behavior. After the walk, create and sketch your own imaginary bird with unique features, a special habitat, and superpowers of its own.
🗓️ Date: Sunday, 7th June
⏰ Time: 6:30 AM – 8:30 AM
📍 Location: Lalbagh Botanical Garden, Bengaluru
🧒 Age Group: 10-15 years
🎨 All materials for the drawing activity will be provided
👉 Limited spots, to register - scan code in poster or use link in bio

title: The perfect murder is just a phone call away!

title: Entry Free
caption_full:
As dusk settles, let the twilight ragas carry you inward. Naad Archana weaves Khayal, Thumri, Tappa, Tarana, and Bhajan into a single offering of sound and devotion. Join us at Indian Heritage Academy on June 15 at 6.30 pm.

title: Dohri Zindagi
caption_full:
Dohri Zindagi
Bangalore tour
18th June to 21st June.
This love story between two women has been winning the hearts of those who have witnessed it.
Based on a short story by Padmashri Vijaydan Detha, and winner of the Gender Bender grant in 2016, this play has travelled to prestigious festivals, across 21 cities and has received the love and warmth of audiences of all ages, demographics.
A story where love defies all odds.
Watch it at one of five shows that we have for you in Bangalore!
We look forward to your support.
Tickets are available on BMS. Link is in my bio.
Poster courtesy: @graphinka4

title: own.
caption_full:

title: Folk music carries the stories, memories and voices of communities across the world.
caption_full:
Folk music carries the stories, memories and voices of communities across the world.
Join Folk and Tonic for a multilingual choral performance that brings together folk traditions from different cultures through harmony, rhythm and collective singing. From familiar melodies to unexpected musical encounters, this evening celebrates the many ways people connect through song.
Featuring:
Anisha Chandy - Soprano
Deepa Jacob - Mezzo Soprano
Sowmya Raghavan - Alto
Nora Alexy - Contra Alto
Noel Prashanth - Keyboard
Varun Krishna – Classical Guitar
Sanket Chakraborty - Percussion and Sound Engineer
🗓 Saturday, 20 June 2026
🕕 6:00 PM
📍 Performance Theatre, Indian Music Experience
🗣️ Multiple Languages
🎟 RSVP via the link in bio
{global folk music, choral performance Bangalore, folk traditions, World Music Weekend, Indian Music Experience Museum}

title: Lal Bagh Phototalk
caption_full:
Lal Bagh Phototalk
📍 Lalbagh Botanical Garden
📅 Sunday, 21 June
🕖 7:00 AM
**Please note: Bringing printed photographs is mandatory.**
**Cameras are discouraged for this event.**
**The exact meeting point will be shared soon.**
The entire idea behind PhotoTalk is to experience photographs away from screens. A print changes the relationship between the viewer and the image. It slows us down. It allows us to notice details, sequencing, scale, and intent in ways that a phone screen rarely can.
Printing is also an act of selection. When you choose to print an image, you're making a conscious decision that this photograph deserves more than a moment in a feed. That decision itself becomes part of the conversation.
PhotoTalk isn't a photowalk, a critique session, or a competition. It's a space where photographers gather around physical photographs and discuss the stories, ideas, processes, and experiences behind them. The print becomes the center of the conversation.
For this reason, we encourage everyone to leave their cameras behind. Lalbagh is simply the venue; the photographs have already been made. This morning is about looking, discussing, and sharing—not shooting.
Whether you're an experienced photographer or have only recently picked up a camera, if there's a photograph you care about, print it and bring it along.
Bring your prints. Bring your curiosity.

title: You don’t need to be a writer.
caption_full:
You don’t need to be a writer.
You don’t need to have the perfect words.
You just need to show up, pick up a pen, and let yourself slow down.
Letters to Strangers is back, in collaboration with @beku.blr Beku - Cafe Bakery Bookstore.
A guided handwritten letter experience with thoughtful prompts, quiet reflection, and the chance to write to someone you’ve just met.
No pressure. No performance. Just pen, paper, presence, and human connection.
Come write what you didn’t know you needed to say.
Sign up now. Link in bio.

title: The Last Mass Leader of Karnataka
caption_full:
📍25 Jun | Thu | 6:30PM
➡️ The Last Mass Leader of Karnataka
Earlier this year, when Siddaramaiah walked into the arched portals of the Vidhana Soudha in Bengaluru, he became the longest serving Chief Minister of Karnataka.
While Siddaramaiah has demitted the chief minister’s office, he will continue to remain a powerful player and is certain to remain an active politician. This panel discussion brings together experts to explore his contributions to the politics and society of Karnataka, from his grounding in socialism to his welfare politics.
Free and open to all ✉️ RSVP on website!

title: Journeys in Sound
caption_full:
📍27 Jun | Sat | 7:00PM
➡️ Journeys in Sound
The rich melodic voice of the sarod, complemented by the evocative textures of the handpan and percussion.
Journeys in Sound is an immersive musical experience by brothers Drupad and Neeraj Mistry. From quiet moments in nature to vibrant cultural encounters, their travels become a sonic voyage across landscapes, memories, and emotions. Each piece unfolds as a conversation between melody and rhythm, carefully crafted compositions giving way to spontaneous musical dialogue, evolving organically in the moment.
Free and open to all ✉️ RSVP on

title: Album
caption_full:
📍25 Jun | Thu | 7:00PM | For Ages 14+
➡️ Album
Language: Hindi
When a daughter stumbles upon an old photo album, she learns the truth behind her parents’ struggles.
Swati Vyas takes to the stage as Manjari. With a meticulous lighting and sound that brings the pages of a static photo album to life, this play is a powerful, heart-wrenching showcase of familial bonds, aging, and urban isolation.
Limited seats RSVP now! Link in Bio ✉️

title: Are Tanjore and Mysore Paintings Different?
caption_full:
📍26 Jun | Fri | 6:30PM
➡️ Are Tanjore and Mysore Paintings Different?
Thanjavur and Mysore are hundreds of kilometres apart; their language, and culture, distinct. When the Vijayanagar Empire fell, its artistic tradition split, taking root in completely different soils and blossoming with the local aesthetic sensibilities.
This conversation brings together art historian Lakshmi Krishnamurthy, curator R G Singh, and designer H S Dharmendra. Together, they discuss and compare the two South Indian artistic traditions.
Free and open to all ✉️ RSVP link in bio!

title: One Step at a Time
caption_full:
📍23 Jun | Tue | 6:30PM
➡️ One Step at a Time
Kaamya Karthikeyan is eighteen. She has summited the highest peak on every continent and skied to the South Pole. All while attending school full time, practicing Bharatanatyam, and competing in debates.
In this conversation, Kaamya speaks with Sunil Chainani – long-distance runner, former Karnataka squash captain, and someone who has spent a lifetime balancing demanding physical goals with a corporate career. Together they explore what it actually takes: the early mornings, the trade-offs, the decision to keep going when stopping was easier.
Free and open to all ✉️ RSVP on the website!

title: Paratattva: The Supreme Essence
caption_full:
📍17 Jun | Wed | 7:00PM
➡️ Paratattva: The Supreme Essence
Encountering Krishna through story, song, and surrender.
This Kuchipudi production unfolds through interconnected vignettes set in Gokula, Vṛndāvana, and landscapes where flowers, longing, and the moon itself become metaphors for Krishna and the blossoming heart of the devotee.
Accompanied by a live orchestra, Paratattva traces how Krishna moves between the intimate and the infinite: child and cosmic force, beloved and protector, playful trickster and Paramātma.
Free and open to all ✉️ RSVP on the website!

title: Dohri Zindagi
caption_full:
📍18 Jun | Thu | 7:00PM | For Ages 18+
➡️ Dohri Zindagi
📍Language: Hindi & Marwadi
Two greedy merchants discover their wives are pregnant, and promise their unborn children to each other in marriage. Both have daughters, but one is secretly raised as a son. The betrothed are married in the traditions of their rural Rajasthani home. What happens on the wedding night, when the truth is revealed?
Dohri Zindagi is based on a short story by Padmashri Vijaydaan Detha, celebrated storyteller of rural Rajasthan, and was adapted for the stage by Neha Singh.
Free and open to all ✉️ RSVP on the website!

title: Voh Subah Hami Se Aayegi | That Dawn That We Will Bring
caption_full:
📍19 Jun | Fri | 6:30PM | For Ages 15+
➡️ Voh Subah Hami Se Aayegi | That Dawn That We Will Bring
This documentary traces the women’s movements in India over four decades: their resistance to violence, their challenges to caste and patriarchal tradition, and the legal and social changes they fought for and won. Spanning regions and communities across the country, the film captures the vibrancy of their leaders, the urgency of the movements and the impact on all our lives.
The screening will be followed by a conversation between the director, Uma Chakravarti and researcher-filmmaker, Madhu Bhushan.
Free and open to all ✉️ RSVP on the website!

title: The Serious Side of Internet Absurdity
caption_full:
📍19 Jun | Fri | 6:30PM
➡️ The Serious Side of Internet Absurdity
The memers, content creators, livestreamers, conspiracy theorists, and influencers we are taught not to take seriously, but somehow cannot peel our eyes away. These so-called unserious corners of the internet may understand contemporary India better than the people we are told to listen to: its aspirations, anxieties, and caste and class contradictions, refracted through the internet’s strangest corners.
Writer and filmmaker Anurag Minus Verma writes about it all in his new book, The Great Indian Brainrot. In this session, he is joined by journalist Priya Ramani. Together, they navigate some of the most absurd and political corners of the internet.
Free and open to all ✉️ RSVP link in bio!

title: Daag
caption_full:
📍11 Jun | Thu | 7:00PM | For Ages 8+
➡️ Daag
Language: Hindi
Bindiya, a tailor, wants to lead a lavish life. She stitches clothes for ladies, aspiring to enter their world. However, her customers short-change her, and impose impossible deadlines. How can she afford to climb the social ladder when those on the upper rungs constantly knock her down? Is it inevitable?
Free and open to all ✉️ RSVP Link in Bio!

title: Of Birds, Bees and Trees
caption_full:
📍7 Jun | Sun | 4:00PM
➡️ Of Birds, Bees and Trees
Birds, plants, trees and insects: discover the natural world, hiding in plain sight.
Bengaluru, like many cities, is actually home to a remarkable diversity of organisms that quietly coexist alongside us: the assassin bug to the banyan trees; the slender loris to the rosy starling. This session begins with a panel discussion featuring speakers from different walks of life, who have each developed a deep engagement with nature in their neighbourhoods. They share personal journeys, practical approaches and memorable experiences from their observations of urban biodiversity.
A short, guided nature walk in and around BIC will follow, giving you a chance to experience firsthand where and how urban wildlife exists.
Free and open to all ✉️ RSVP link in bio!

title: Green Poet
caption_full:
📍7 Jun | Sun | 6:00PM
➡️ Poet of the Forest
If there is one literary figure in India who can be described as a ‘Green Poet’, it is Kuvempu. While this passion for nature manifests itself in every genre of his writing – poetry, plays, short stories, and essays, his fiction em-bodies nature in the most organic, most complex, and most complete manner.
Kuvempu spoke about his novels as, “Cosmic vision brooding on local things.” Does Kuvempu represent nature as a benign and benevolent power, or does he see nature as a callous, malevolent force in his narrative? How does Kuvempu imagine the relationship between nature and the predicament of the human world? How do we read Kuvempu’s relevance when we are faced with the palpable challenges of climate change?
Free and open to all ✉️ RSVP on website!

title: On Her Own Terms
caption_full:
📍6 Jun | Sat | 11:00AM
➡️ On Her Own Terms
Asha Bhosle was one of India’s most iconic playback singers, one of the most recorded voices in history. Her repertoire spanned cabaret numbers to ghazals, pop experiments and timeless classics. She was also a woman who navigated personal struggles, artistic competition, and reinvention with resilience and flair.
This discussion will trace her evolution as an artist and public figure through the decades of change in Indian cinema and music industries. Interwoven through the conversation are live renditions of some of her memorable songs.
Free and open to all ✉️ Limited Seats RSVP Now! Link in Bio!

title: Chathurdasa Ragamalika: Reimagined
caption_full:
📍30 May | Sat | 7:00PM
➡️ Chathurdasa Ragamalika: Reimagined
Fourteen ragams, one composition.
Muthuswami Dikshitar’s Sri Vishwanatham Bhajeham is one of Carnatic music’s great architectural achievements. The transitions are seamless. Each ragam retains its distinct identity.
This concert builds upon Dikshitar’s ragamalika, introducing pieces by different composers across languages and compositional forms. The same ragam, heard through different hands — violin, veenai, mridangam, ghatam — reveals new layers with every listening.
Free and open to all ✉️ RSVP on the website!
